record.field: Unleash is the leading open-source feature management platform for controlling how and when features reach your users.

Unleash is the leading open-source feature management platform for controlling how and when features reach your users.

It lets engineering teams decouple deployment from release through feature flags, enabling gradual rollouts, instant kill switches, A/B testing and targeted delivery without shipping new code. Built on Node.js with PostgreSQL, it exposes an API-first design and 15+ official SDKs so flags can be evaluated across virtually any language or framework. Its privacy-by-design architecture keeps end-user data inside your own infrastructure.

Key features
  • Feature flags with gradual (percentage) rollouts and instant kill switches
  • Flexible activation strategies, custom strategies and user segmentation
  • API-first design with 15+ official SDKs for backend and frontend
  • Unleash Edge for scaling flag evaluation to many frontend clients
  • Insights and stale-flag management to control technical debt
  • Audit logging, RBAC and SSO for governance and access control
Best for

Development and platform teams that want to ship continuously, roll out features safely, run experiments and manage feature lifecycle from self-hosted infrastructure with full data control.

services: unleash: image: unleashorg/unleash-server:7.4.0 restart: unless-stopped environment: DATABASE_URL: postgres://{{ DB_USER }}:${DB_PASSWORD}@db/{{ DB_NAME }} DATABASE_SSL: 'false' LOG_LEVEL: warn depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy healthcheck: test: wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:4242/health || exit 1 interval: 1s timeout: 1m retries: 5 start_period: 15s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:4242' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: postgres:15 restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_DB: {{ DB_NAME }} POSTGRES_USER: {{ DB_USER }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD} volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD - pg_isready - --username={{ DB_USER }} - --host=127.0.0.1 - --port=5432 interval: 2s timeout: 1m retries: 5 start_period: 10s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
